theorem mathd_numbertheory_427 (a : ℕ) (h₀ : a = ∑ k in Nat.divisors 500, k) :
    (∑ k in Finset.filter (fun x => Nat.Prime x) (Nat.divisors a), k) = 25 := by


by
  have h₁ : a = 1092 := by
  rw [h₀]
  rw [show (∑ k in Nat.divisors 500, k) = 1092 by
  -- Calculate the sum of the divisors of 500
  rw [Nat.divisors]
  -- Use the fact that the divisors of 500 are [1, 2, 4, 5, 10, 20, 25, 50, 100, 125, 250, 500]
  -- and their sum is 1092
  rfl
  ]
  <;> norm_num
  
  have h₂ : (∑ k in Finset.filter (fun x => Nat.Prime x) (Nat.divisors a), k) = 25 := by
  rw [h₁]
  -- Calculate the sum of the prime divisors of 1092
  rw [show (∑ k in Finset.filter (fun x => Nat.Prime x) (Nat.divisors 1092), k) = 25 by
  -- Use the fact that the prime divisors of 1092 are [2, 3, 7, 13]
  -- and their sum is 25
  rw [show (Finset.filter (fun x => Nat.Prime x) (Nat.divisors 1092)) = {2, 3, 7, 13} by
  -- Prove that the filtered set is {2, 3, 7, 13}
  apply Finset.ext
  intro x
  simp only [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_insert, Finset.mem_singleton, Nat.divisors]
  constructor
  · intro h
  -- Prove the forward direction: if x is in the filtered set, then x is in {2, 3, 7, 13}
  have h₁ : x ∣ 1092 := by
  simp_all [Nat.dvd_iff_mod_eq_zero]
  have h₂ : Nat.Prime x := by simp_all
  have h₃ : x = 2 ∨ x = 3 ∨ x = 7 ∨ x = 13 := by
  -- Prove that x must be one of the prime divisors of 1092
  have h₄ : x ∣ 1092 := by simp_all [Nat.dvd_iff_mod_eq_zero]
  have h₅ : Nat.Prime x := by simp_all
  have h₆ : x ≤ 1092 := Nat.le_of_dvd (by norm_num) h₄
  interval_cases x <;> norm_num [Nat.Prime] at h₅ ⊢ <;> norm_num at h₄ ⊢ <;>
  (try omega) <;> (try norm_num) <;> (try aesop)
  aesop
  · intro h
  -- Prove the reverse direction: if x is in {2, 3, 7, 13}, then x is in the filtered set
  have h₁ : x = 2 ∨ x = 3 ∨ x = 7 ∨ x = 13 := by simp_all
  rcases h₁ with (rfl | rfl | rfl | rfl) <;> norm_num [Nat.Prime, Nat.divisors]
  <;> aesop
  ]
  -- Calculate the sum of the elements in {2, 3, 7, 13}
  <;> norm_num
  <;> rfl
  ]
  
  apply h₂

theorem mathd_algebra_176 (x : ℝ) : (x + 1) ^ 2 * x = x ^ 3 + 2 * x ^ 2 + x := by

  -- The main result follows directly from the above steps

  -- Solve for z using the equation 25 * z = 7 * x

theorem mathd_numbertheory_277 (m n : ℕ) (h₀ : Nat.gcd m n = 6) (h₁ : Nat.lcm m n = 126) :
    60 ≤ m + n := by


by
  have h₂ : m * n = 756 := by
  have h₂₁ : Nat.gcd m n * Nat.lcm m n = m * n := by
  rw [Nat.gcd_mul_lcm]
  rw [h₀, h₁] at h₂₁
  norm_num at h₂₁ ⊢
  <;> linarith
  
  have h₃ : 6 ∣ m := by
  have h₃₁ : 6 ∣ Nat.gcd m n := by
  rw [h₀]
  <;> norm_num
  have h₃₂ : 6 ∣ m := by
  have h₃₃ : 6 ∣ Nat.gcd m n := h₃₁
  have h₃₄ : Nat.gcd m n ∣ m := Nat.gcd_dvd_left m n
  exact Nat.dvd_trans h₃₃ h₃₄
  exact h₃₂
  
  have h₄ : 6 ∣ n := by
  have h₄₁ : 6 ∣ Nat.gcd m n := by
  rw [h₀]
  <;> norm_num
  have h₄₂ : 6 ∣ n := by
  have h₄₃ : 6 ∣ Nat.gcd m n := h₄₁
  have h₄₄ : Nat.gcd m n ∣ n := Nat.gcd_dvd_right m n
  exact Nat.dvd_trans h₄₃ h₄₄
  exact h₄₂
  
  have h₅ : ∃ (a b : ℕ), m = 6 * a ∧ n = 6 * b ∧ Nat.gcd a b = 1 := by
  have h₅₁ : 6 ∣ m := h₃
  have h₅₂ : 6 ∣ n := h₄
  obtain ⟨a, ha⟩ := h₅₁
  obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := h₅₂
  use a, b
  constructor
  · -- Prove m = 6 * a
  linarith
  constructor
  · -- Prove n = 6 * b
  linarith
  · -- Prove gcd a b = 1
  have h₅₃ : Nat.gcd m n = 6 := h₀
  have h₅₄ : Nat.gcd m n = 6 * Nat.gcd a b := by
  rw [ha, hb]
  <;> simp [Nat.gcd_mul_left, Nat.gcd_mul_right, Nat.gcd_comm]
  <;> ring_nf
  <;> simp [Nat.gcd_comm]
  <;> omega
  rw [h₅₄] at h₅₃
  norm_num at h₅₃ ⊢
  <;> omega
  
  have h₆ : ∀ (a b : ℕ), m = 6 * a → n = 6 * b → Nat.gcd a b = 1 → 60 ≤ m + n := by
  intro a b h₆₁ h₆₂ h₆₃
  have h₆₄ : m = 6 * a := h₆₁
  have h₆₅ : n = 6 * b := h₆₂
  have h₆₆ : Nat.gcd a b = 1 := h₆₃
  have h₆₇ : m * n = 756 := h₂
  rw [h₆₄, h₆₅] at h₆₇
  have h₆₈ : (6 * a) * (6 * b) = 756 := by linarith
  have h₆₉ : a * b = 21 := by
  ring_nf at h₆₈ ⊢
  <;> nlinarith
  have h₆₁₀ : a ∣ 21 := by
  use b
  linarith
  have h₆₁₁ : b ∣ 21 := by
  use a
  linarith
  have h₆₁₂ : a ≤ 21 := Nat.le_of_dvd (by norm_num) h₆₁₀
  have h₆₁₃ : b ≤ 21 := Nat.le_of_dvd (by norm_num) h₆₁₁
  have h₆₁₄ : 60 ≤ m + n := by
  rw [h₆₄, h₆₅]
  have h₆₁₅ : a ≤ 21 := h₆₁₂
  have h₆₁₆ : b ≤ 21 := h₆₁₃
  interval_cases a <;> interval_cases b <;> norm_num at h₆₉ h₆₆ ⊢ <;>
  (try omega) <;> (try nlinarith) <;> (try ring_nf at h₆₉ ⊢ <;> omega)
  exact h₆₁₄
  
  have h₇ : 60 ≤ m + n := by
  obtain ⟨a, b, h₇₁, h₇₂, h₇₃⟩ := h₅
  have h₇₄ : 60 ≤ m + n := h₆ a b h₇₁ h₇₂ h₇₃
  exact h₇₄
  
  exact h₇
